name: Mexican Pork-Garlic Stew with Hominy
recipeIngredient: 1 large onion, cut into medium dice
3 pounds boneless pork shoulder, or butt, cut into 1-inch cubes
1 quart beef stock, or low-salt beef broth
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large pan, sauté the chorizo over moderate heat until it loses its pink color, about 3 minutes. Remove with a slotted spoon.
2. Sauté onion and garlic in chorizo fat over moderate heat for 10 minutes.
3. Add sherry and cook for 4-5 minutes, until the liquid evaporates. Stir in the chorizo and set aside.
4. Heat olive oil in a heavy-bottomed pot. When hot but not smoking, brown pork cubes on all sides.
5. Return the pork and chorizo mixture to the pot, add spices, oregano, and beef stock. Bring to a boil over high heat, scraping the bottom of the pot to remove any particles.
6. Reduce heat to moderate and cook for 2 hours or until meat is tender. Add hominy and lime juice and cook an additional 10 minutes.
7. Mix in green onions and cilantro just before serving. Serve hot. We usually eat it over rice.
